message:
fix(sync): prevent unbounded waiter queue growth in Mutex

Previously, poll() would push a new waker to the waiters queue on every
call, even when already registered. In high-contention scenarios with
spurious wakes, this caused unbounded queue growth.

Fix: Track registration state and only push waker once per lock attempt.
- LockFuture now only pushes waker when !self.registered
- OwnedLockFuture mirrors the same pattern with registered field
- Removed verbose comments explaining the problem (code is self-documenting)

Trade-off: If waker changes between polls (rare), stale waker is used.
This is harmless - another waiter gets woken instead.

A more sophisticated implementation would use intrusive linked lists
with proper deregistration on drop, but this simple fix prevents the
immediate issue without architectural changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
